pubmed_links: [Bhatt et al. (2013) Npas4: A neuronal transcription factor with a key role in social and cognitive functions relevant to developmental disorders](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/23029555/); [Bhatt et al. (2021) Molecular characterisation of rare loss-of-function NPAS3 and NPAS4 variants identified in individuals with neurodevelopmental disorders](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33758288/); [Jaramillo et al. (2019) Downregulation of Npas4 in parvalbumin interneurons and cognitive deficits after neonatal NMDA receptor blockade: relevance for schizophrenia](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/30792384/)
summary: NPAS4 is an activity-dependent neuronal transcription factor that regulates excitatory-inhibitory synaptic balance, and its deficiency in mice produces social, cognitive, and sensorimotor gating deficits mirroring autism and schizophrenia symptomatology; rare loss-of-function NPAS4 variants have been identified in individuals with developmental delay and intellectual disability. (emerging evidence)
